name: Grilled Pork Tacos & Papaya Salsa
recipeInstructions:
Papaya Salsa
1. Peel, seed, and cut papaya into half-inch cubes.
2. Chop red chili (remove seeds).
3. Chop red onion.
4. Chop red bell pepper.
5. Chop fresh mint leaves.
6. Squeeze in 2 tablespoons of lime juice.
Pork Mixture
1. Cut pork boneless center loin into strips measuring 1/4 inch by 1/4 inch.
2. Chop fresh papaya and pineapple.
3. Warm flour tortillas (6 or 7 inches in diameter).
4. Shred 1 1/2 cups of Monterey Jack cheese.
5. Melt 2 tablespoons of margarine or butter.
1. Cook pork in a 10-inch skillet over medium heat for about 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until no longer pink. Drain excess fat.
2. Stir in papaya and pineapple; cook until hot.
3. Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C).
4. Spoon about 1/4 cup of the pork mixture onto half of each tortilla. Place approximately 2 tablespoons of cheese on top.
5. Fold tortillas over the filling.
6. Arrange five filled tortillas in an ungreased jelly roll pan, covering them with melted margarine.
7. Bake uncovered for about 10 minutes or until lightly golden brown. Repeat with remaining tacos.
Salsa: Mix all ingredients together and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes before serving. Makes 3 cups of salsa.
